SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper provides a coherent technical development: derivation of the truncated-importance-with-bias-correction estimator (Equations 7–9), a trust-region gradient transform with closed-form solution (Equations 11–12), and a proof sketch showing the Retrace operator equivalence (Proposition 1, Appendix C). Empirical methodology includes reasonable baselines (A3C, DQN, Prioritized Replay, trajectory-truncated IS) and ablations across Atari and MuJoCo, and hyper-parameter search is reported. Minor concerns remain: the contraction proof in Appendix C assumes finite state/action spaces while the method is applied to continuous control, and the experimental results lack reported statistical significance tests (only means/std or best-of-N are shown), which slightly weakens the empirical support. Overall the math and algorithms appear correct and consistent, with these limited caveats.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper combines several ideas into a single, practically useful algorithm: truncated importance sampling with a principled bias-correction decomposition, Stochastic Dueling Networks for consistent Q/V estimation in continuous actions, and an efficient average-policy-based trust-region update. While each component builds on prior work (Retrace, dueling nets, TRPO, off-policy PG), the particular combination and the new derivations (e.g. showing Retrace as an instance of the truncation+bias trick and the closed-form trust-region gradient transform) are novel and non-trivial. This represents notable, pragmatic novelty rather than a wholly new paradigm.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well structured (intro, background, discrete/continuous methods, experiments, theory, appendix) and most notation and equations are clearly stated (key equations numbered and referenced: (5),(7)–(10),(11)–(12),(13)–(16),(20)). Figures and pseudo-code (Algorithms 1–3) support reproducibility and the ablation/experimental descriptions are informative. The provided text does contain some duplicated/repeated paragraphs in the supplied document and a few dense derivations that may challenge some readers, but overall exposition and organization are strong.
